text: In 476 the Germanic chieftain Odoacer deposed Romulus Augustulus, ending the Western Roman Empire. Eastern Empire continued from Constantinople for another millennium.
At Runnymede in 1215 King John of England sealed the Magna Carta, establishing the principle that the king is subject to law. It seeded constitutional limits on royal power.
In October 1347 Genoese ships fleeing the siege of Caffa brought plague to Messina in Sicily. Within five years the Black Death killed roughly a third of Europe.
The Peace of Westphalia in 1648 ended the Thirty Years War and established the modern principle of state sovereignty, the basis of the international system.
On 4 July 1776 the Continental Congress in Philadelphia adopted the Declaration of Independence, asserting that governments derive their just powers from the consent of the governed.
The Meiji Restoration of 1868 abolished the Tokugawa shogunate and re-centered political power on the emperor, launching Japan's rapid industrialization.
The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand in Sarajevo on 28 June 1914 triggered the alliance cascade that became the First World War.
On 9 November 1989 East German authorities opened the Berlin Wall, marking the symbolic collapse of the Eastern Bloc and the end of the Cold War order.
